title: Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph
description:
You have a graph of `n` nodes. You are given an integer `n` and an array `edges` where `edges[i] = [aᵢ, bᵢ]` indicates that there is an edge between `aᵢ` and `bᵢ` in the graph.

Return the number of connected components in the graph.

hint_plan.level_1: Break down the core operation in Number of Connected Components in an Undirected Graph.
hint_plan.level_2: Look for the data structure or traversal pattern that avoids repeated work.
hint_plan.level_3: Use the constraints and examples to narrow the intended time complexity.
hint_plan.level_4: Implement the direct approach that maintains the required invariant step by step.

canonical_solution_summary: Implement the direct approach that maintains the required invariant step by step.
hints_per_sentence: Start by defining the function with parameters n and edges.
Initialize a list for parent pointers where each node initially points to itself.
Create a rank list to track tree heights, all starting at zero.
Define a helper function to find the root of a node with path compression.
Check if the node is its own parent; if not, proceed to compress the path.
Recursively set the parent to the root and return it for efficiency.
Define another helper function to union two nodes by their roots.
Find the root of the first node using the find function.
Find the root of the second node similarly.
If the roots are the same, the nodes are already connected, so do nothing.
Compare ranks to decide which root becomes the parent to keep trees balanced.
If the first root has lower rank, attach it to the second root.
If the second root has lower rank, attach it to the first root.
If ranks are equal, choose one as parent and increment its rank.
Increment the rank of the chosen parent to maintain balance.
Iterate through each edge in the edges list.
Apply the union operation to connect the nodes of each edge.
Initialize an empty set to store unique root identifiers.
Loop through all nodes from 0 to n-1.
Add the root of each node to the set to count distinct components.
Return the size of the set as the number of connected components.
final_explanation: This solution uses Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union) to efficiently track connected components. By initializing each node as its own parent and unioning nodes for each edge, it builds sets of connected nodes. Finally, it counts unique roots to determine the number of components, leveraging path compression and union by rank for optimal performance.
